parsed_review.summary: CP00-336 is a descriptor-only RP10.P07 failure tail slice pack (M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail S21-S22 plus M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head S01-S08, 10 units, risk A). The pack-level claim is honestly production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / implementation_layer:descriptor; it does NOT overclaim production or enterprise trust, so the production_ready gate is respected. Implementation is genuinely descriptor-only: freezeCp336Result (service.js:2282) pins all runtime/write/leak flags to false; the cumulative ROW_EXTRAS chain supplies the security fields (claude_final_approval_claimed:false, human_final_approval_required:true from CP334:2073-2074; cross_tenant_access_allowed:false, permission_decision_detail_included:false, expected_outcome rejected/denied_customer_safe from CP333:1922-1928); and validateIntakeCoreCp336FailureTailSliceDescriptor (validators.js:3591) strictly enforces them. Coverage/deliverable distribution matches the manifest, baseline, and prompt (M05:2+M06:8=10; claude_review:1/implementation:1/failure_recovery:7/security_audit:1). The contract is in sync — the validator script deepEquals current_pack, no_write_attestation, cp336_requirements, cp336_no_write_attestation, and failure_tail_slice_descriptor against in-code objects, and all targets exist and match. Tests (model.test.js:1090-1136) cover coverage, descriptor-only rows, security fields, and packet/handoff routing to CP00-337. Authority boundary intact: Claude read-only (Read/Grep/Glob), claude_is_final_approval:false, human final approval required for enterprise trust / runtime opening (CP00-337); Hermes appears only as a deterministic H10 evidence descriptor (receipt_shape descriptor_only_intake_failure_tail_slice_no_write), never product runtime. CP178+ invalid-review blocker list is complete and matches; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON. No P0/P1/P2 defects found. One P3 informational note records that downstream review artifacts are expectedly pending at this read-only step, so this PASS is a reviewer signal only and does not authorize pack closeout.

findings.title: Downstream review/closeout artifacts pending — expected at read-only review step; PASS does not authorize closeout
findings.details: At this read-only review step (step 3 of the 9-step hardening sequence), the downstream artifacts claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json (all listed in required_pack_artifacts, manifest lines 506-512) plus raw-output.json and review-receipt.json are not yet present, and pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending' with valid_review_receipts empty (manifest line 573). This is the correct state, not a defect: those artifacts are produced FROM this review and from the post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:review:normalize / receipt:validate, closeout-pack:validate, closeout-pack-plan:generate, git diff --check --cached) that command-evidence.json notes are run before commit. Recorded to make explicit that this PASS verdict is an independent read-only reviewer signal only and does not itself close the pack or the enterprise trust claim — human final approval remains required per the authority boundary.
findings.recommendation: Proceed through the remaining hardening steps (normalize -> receipt validate -> finding adjudication -> construction inspection -> final closeout validation) and obtain human final approval before treating CP00-336 as closed. Do not commit until claude-review-result.json and the post-review validations exist and pass. No implementation change is required.
